Isatis coerulea, also known as Indigo plant, is a plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family and is known for its blue dye-producing leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Isatis coerulea include indole alkaloids, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Isatis coerulea is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, bronchitis, and skin conditions.
